what_it_does: Documents a specific local model variant — the GGUF Q3_K_M build of Qwen3.6-27B — including its metadata, intended use cases, Ollama registration status, launch config, quantisation trade-offs, and a list of unverified performance questions.
observations: File size 12.7 GB / disk snapshot 13 GB
Quantised to GGUF Q3_K_M to fit 16 GB VRAM
Registered with Ollama but never load-tested or benchmarked
Positioned as the per-request quality option versus the throughput-optimised 35B MoE variant
Includes a known-good curl launch snippet
Explicitly notes that single-stream latency is expected to be high and quality-vs-latency trade-off is unmeasured
standouts: A large dense model is held as the quality option while a MoE sibling is the throughput option
The file repeatedly flags that the model is downloaded but completely unmeasured
Q3_K_M is chosen specifically to leave KV-cache headroom on 16 GB VRAM
conventions: YAML frontmatter with type, id, status, runtime, ollama_name, date_added, and tags
Wikilink-style cross-references to sibling model entries and operations pages
'At a glance' metadata table followed by Source, Fits these needs, Tested performance, Quirks, Open at this entry, and Connects to sections
Uses a curl example as the canonical launch config
concerns: Claims the model is 'operational' despite the same section stating it was never load-tested or benchmarked
Performance section is entirely speculative ('expected slow', 'likely the slowest') without measurements
Specific context-length limit and KV headroom are left as open questions
Date 2026-05-27 is in the future relative to training knowledge, which may be a placeholder or typo
notes: A ledger card for a downloaded-but-unmeasured dense 27B GGUF model, mainly useful as a placeholder for future benchmarking and as a quality-versus-throughput contrast to the MoE variant.
questions: What context length does Ollama actually expose for this model on the local substrate?
How much KV-cache headroom remains when loading 12.7 GB into 16 GB VRAM?
What is the measured single-stream tok/s?
How does quality compare to the 4B AWQ baseline on the owner's typical queries?
Does long-context recall hold at multi-thousand-token depths?
Is tool calling reliable through the Ollama/GGUF path?
purpose_vs_actual: Stated purpose is to record the model, its fit, and how to launch it; the file does that, but the 'Tested performance' section contains no actual measurements, so the ledger entry is more of a registration record plus a measurement checklist than a validated substrate choice.
apparent_themes: Local model inventory and substrate fit
Quality-oriented dense models versus throughput-oriented MoE models
intention_signals: To register a candidate high-quality local LLM option, explain why it was chosen (dense 27B for per-request quality), and flag every measurement that still needs to happen before it can be trusted as a default.
novelty: Explicitly contrasts a dense 27B variant with a same-family MoE variant and assigns them different optimisation goals (quality vs throughput) within the same ledger.
contribution: A structured registration record for a specific local model variant, including its intended use cases, launch command, quantisation rationale, and a checklist of unverified performance questions that a fused system can use to track what remains to be measured.
summary_for_embedding: Ledger entry for the downloaded but unmeasured Qwen3.6-27B GGUF Q3_K_M dense text-only model registered with Ollama, intended for single-stream high-quality reasoning and long-context document QA on a 16 GB VRAM substrate.
intention_for_embedding: To document a candidate dense large model, its operational registration status, and the measurements needed to decide whether its quality justifies its latency cost.
